Defendant 2479 Marin Street, Llc'S Motion To Contest Good Faith Settlement Of Vina Cab, Llc, Tam Dinh Nguyen, Tim Dinh Nguyen
Off Calendar Mar-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants Vina Cab, LLC, Tam Dinh Nguyen, and Tim Dinh Nguyen's application for good faith determination of their settlement with plaintiff Erin Kennedy-Andress is granted. Vina Cab and the Nguyens satisfied their initial burden of providing sufficient information on all of the Tech-Bilt factors and defendant 2479 Marin Street, LLC has not shown that the settlement is so far outside the Tech-Bilt "ballpark" that the settlement should be deemed to be made in bad faith. All defendants agree that, while the plaintiff's injuries are serious, there is a significant prospect that plaintiff will not be able to prove any of her claims and/or her damages will be considerably reduced due to plaintiff's own fault for her injuries. Plaintiff's demand for $100,000,000 is not a realistic approximation of her recovery given her much more modest special damages. Based on the attenuated connection between the conduct of Vina Cab and the Nguyens and plaintiff's injuries, the settlement amount of $50,000 is well within the range of Vina Cab and the Nguyens' likely proportional liability and thus meets the Tech-Bilt test for being made in good faith. (1) Order signed for both motions. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Defendant 2479 Marin Street, Llc'S Motion To Contest Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Mar-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants SF Taxi-Cab Co. and Igor Kopetman's application for good faith determination of their settlement with plaintiff Erin Kennedy-Andress is granted. SF Taxi and Mr. Kopetman satisfied their initial burden of providing sufficient information on all of the Tech-Bilt factors and defendant 2479 Marin Street, LLC has not shown that the settlement is so far outside the Tech-Bilt "ballpark" that the settlement should be deemed to be made in bad faith. All defendants agree that, while the plaintiff's injuries are serious, there is a significant prospect that plaintiff will not be able to prove any of her claims and/or her damages will be considerably reduced due to plaintiff's own fault for her injuries. Plaintiff's demand for $100,000,000 is not a realistic approximation of her recovery given her much more modest special damages. Based on the attenuated connection between the conduct of SF Taxi and Mr. Kopetman and plaintiff's injuries, the settlement amount of $50,000 is well within the range of SF Taxi and Mr. Kopetman's likely proportional liability and thus meets the Tech-Bilt test for being made in good faith. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or, In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication Of Issues/ Notice
Off Calendar Oct-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS TIM NGUYEN, TAM NGUYEN and VINA CAB, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or, In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication Of Issues is DENIED in their entirety. Plaintiff Erin Kennedy-Andress presented evidence that raises a triable issue whether defendants had control or possession of the premises where plaintiff fell. (Responses to UMF 6 and 11). Defendants' objections based on the D'Amico doctrine to that evidence are overruled because defendants have not shown that plaintiff's evidence unequivocally contradicted her deposition. Defendants' argument that they owed no duty to plaintiff because she was a sublessee at the time of her fall is contrary to California law. Defendants did not satisfy their summary judgment burden showing that plaintiff's injuries were caused by an open and obvious condition of the premises or that plaintiff's own negligence was the sole cause of her injuries. None of the facts stated in defendants' separate statement establish either of these contentions. Plaintiff's request for a continuance per CCP 437c(h) is moot. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk
